arts.ttl: Leeds United chain reaction could hit best weapon vs Coventry as dry spell looms
arts.sum: Tanaka has been promising in his cameos from the bench, but Farke has singled Rothwell out more than once for praise this season. Unlike Struijk, Junior Firpo was in full training on Thursday and Farke was hopeful, if the Dominican’s body reacted positively, he would be ready for today.

arts.ttl: New Elland Road pressure verdict as Daniel Farke faces Leeds United challenge
arts.sum: Leeds United's plans to increase the capaciy of Elland Road to 53,000 will only pile on the pressure for Daniel Farke to get the Whites back to the Premier League - but the German has been backed to rise to the challenge. “This is fantastic news for Leeds United, but what it does is put pressure on Daniel Farke and his team to get promotion.

arts.ttl: Leeds United may need drastic free agent cover if double surgery concern materialises
arts.sum: Leeds United have Max Wöber potentially looking at knee surgery at the moment while Pascal Struijk may have suffered a setback in the adductor that needed an operation last season to recover. As for Struijk, he has suffered an injury to his adductor, a somewhat alarming development after he spent the entire second half of 23/24 sidelined with the same issue, eventually needing surgery himself:

arts.ttl: Daniel Farke harbours fresh Patrick Bamford hope for Leeds United ahead of Coventry City
arts.sum: Experience will be a welcome boost to Leeds United when Daniel Farke can rely on Patrick Bamford, Manor Solomon and Daniel James again. Or Largie Ramazani, more or less his first start in English senior football in the last game, of course, he can't be there in his prime with his best possible performance.

arts.ttl: Daniel Farke comments should spark re-think on Leeds United “wrecking ball” that Bielsa loved
arts.sum: Leeds United manager Daniel Farke responded to a question regarding the lack of playing time for a player like Joe Gelhardt and there should be more of an effort made to utilise his obvious talents. Finding a place for Gelhardt, for example, is easier said than done to get him on the pitch when there are a plethora of other options in Farke’s squad – such as Joel Piroe coming off the bench twice this season to score and clinch 2-0 wins.

arts.ttl: Major double Leeds United injury boost pre-Coventry teased on social media
arts.sum: Leeds United defensive duo Pascal Struijk and Junior Firpo have suggested they are available for today’s game against Coventry City after recent injury setbacks. Included in that was Pascal Struijk, suffering an adductor issue this week in training, missing out at Thorp Arch on Thursday, and Junior Firpo, whose MCL problems have persisted following the win over Cardiff City.

arts.ttl: Ross McCormack saved Leeds United with 2014 £11m exit
arts.sum: Leeds United sold Ross McCormack back in 2014 to Fulham amid controversy over Massimo Cellino’s ownership, but the club was in far worse shape than it appeared at the time. It wasn’t a deal that went down well at the time with Leeds selling their best player by some distance to a league rival, but McCormack has now detailed to Open Goal how his exit essentially saved the club:

arts.ttl: Leeds United squad revealed for Coventry City with one major doubt and three players sidelined
arts.sum: “It's a difficult team because even if they're in a bad position in the table, they're still a play-off team so it's difficult,” said the Frenchman. Leeds United are acutely aware of the threat Coventry City will pose at Elland Road today.

arts.ttl: Leeds United wanted £44m-rated PL star, he’s managed 24G/A since start of last season
arts.sum: Wolves star Matheus Cunha was a target for Leeds United during their time in the Premier League, and his form at Molineux proved it was a mistake for the Whites not to sign him. Leeds tried to sign Matheus Cunha from Hertha Berlin, he’s ended up at Wolves instead

arts.ttl: 'Leaving my comfort zone' Leeds United summer decision having knock-on effect
arts.sum: Moving to Bulgaria has taken Liam Cooper out of his comfort zone, he admits, after fulfilling his childhood dream with Leeds United for nearly 10 years. Stiliyan Petrov, arguably the best known Bulgarian footballer in the UK, and Ilia Gruev, Cooper’s former Leeds team-mate, were crucial in reassuring the centre-back about the move.
